text:
Okay. There once was a tiny mouse named Oliver. He lived in a cozy little burrow deep within the bramble patch. Oliver loved making little nests for his friends.

One sunny morning, Oliver noticed something odd. His usual collection of eggs had vanished! Panicking, he scampered outside. There nestled among the ferns was a hen pecking frantically.

"Oh dear!" Oliver squeaked. "Do you think you could help?"

The hen blinked slowly. She eyed Oliver closely. After a moment, she chirped softly and beckoned him closer.

Oliver scampered up. The hen gathered some seeds and spilled some grain onto the grass. Together, they fed.

Afternoon turned to twilight. Still, no eggs appeared.

"A visitor," murmured the hen.

They watched a squirrel slip in and out with something brown.

"Hmmm," said the hen thoughtfully. She chowed down a bit.

Night fell as they retreated into the warmth of Oliver's burrow.
